headline: Visualizing Category Theory Through Ordered Sets
lede: Abstract mathematical structures often feel inaccessible until grounded in visual logic. This guide maps the relationships between linear and partial orders, offering a concrete entry point into the mechanics of categorical products and coproducts.

headline: Remembering Michael O. Rabin, Master of Complexity
lede: The architect of nondeterministic automata and pioneer of primality testing has passed away at age 94. His contributions remain the bedrock upon which our modern cryptographic world is built.

headline: The Visual Legacy of the Amiga Era
lede: Before modern GPUs, the Commodore Amiga pushed the boundaries of what was possible on a home computer. This digital archive restores the technical innovations and artistic demonstrations that defined an era of early multimedia.

headline: Kdenlive Prioritizes Stability in 2026
lede: The development cycle shifts away from rapid feature expansion to prioritize core infrastructure. Keyframing refactoring and workflow refinements define the current release trajectory for the KDE video editor.

headline: Floating-Point Equality Beyond Epsilon Hacks
lede: Standard epsilon comparisons are often a crutch for poor architectural design. By embracing IEEE-754 compliance, developers can write more predictable logic that survives the rigors of complex computational geometry.

headline: Anthropic Introduces Claude Design for Conversational Prototyping
lede: The integration of AI into design workflows promises to accelerate the transition from concept to production. Claude Design automates the generation of prototypes while maintaining strict adherence to existing enterprise design systems.

headline: A New Calculator for Disjoint Interval Arithmetic
lede: Standard arithmetic often falters when division by zero enters the equation. This TypeScript implementation uses interval union arithmetic to maintain closed systems and provide reliable calculations for complex, disjoint sets.

headline: Quantifying the Cost of Claude 4.7 Tokenization
lede: Recent updates to the tokenizer have quietly shifted the economics of AI-driven coding. Users are seeing a substantial increase in consumption for standard workflows, raising questions about long-term dependency costs.

headline: Restoring Trust in the Emacs Ecosystem
lede: New security defaults in Emacs often break legitimate workflows, forcing developers to bypass essential protections. The new trust-manager package attempts to restore usability by introducing manageable, just-in-time authorizations.

headline: The Lingering Toxicity of Lunar Regolith
lede: Apollo astronauts encountered more than just airless terrain; they brought back evidence of abrasive, sharp-edged dust. Scientists are now studying the respiratory hazards of this planetary material to ensure future lunar explorers remain safe.
